exEn: Wǒ shì xuéshēng — I am a student
strokeTip: Start with a diagonal stroke, then the hook, then the short horizontal — imagine an arm pointing to yourself.

exEn: Nǐ hǎo ma — How are you?
strokeTip: Person radical (亻) on the left, then 尔 on the right. Left side first, always.

exEn: Tā shì wǒ de péngyǒu — He is my friend
strokeTip: Person radical (亻) left, then 也 right. A person and 'also' — he is also a person.

exEn: Tā hěn piàoliang — She is very beautiful
strokeTip: Woman radical (女) left, then 也 right. Same sound as 他 but written with 女.
Woman (女) + child (子). Crossed strokes first, then the base line, then the child on the right.

exEn: Wǒ shì lǎoshī — I am a teacher
strokeTip: Sun (日) on top, then the long diagonal stroke below. Top to bottom.

exEn: Wǒ bù zhīdào — I don't know
strokeTip: Horizontal stroke, then vertical down, then two short strokes. Like a bird's foot pointing down.

exEn: Zhège rén hěn hǎo — This person is very good
strokeTip: Two strokes: left-falling then right-falling, like two legs of a person standing.

exEn: Zhège hěn dà — This is very big
strokeTip: Person (人) with arms spread wide — horizontal stroke added on top to show size.
Box first, then the vertical line through the center — hitting the exact middle.
Outer box first, then jade (玉) inside, then close the box. A jewel inside borders.
Short horizontal on bottom, then long horizontal, then short vertical pointing up.
Long horizontal first, then short horizontal below it, then vertical with a dot — pointing down.
Horizontal, then the central cross, then two side strokes. Picture someone walking toward you.

exEn: Wǒ qù xuéxiào — I go to school
strokeTip: Horizontal at top, then box-like middle, then a long vertical with a flick — going away.

exEn: Nǐ shuō shénme — What did you say?
strokeTip: Speech radical (讠) left, then 兑 right. The speech radical is two dots + a vertical — like sound coming out.
Hand (手) radical on top shielding eyes, then eye (目) below — a hand shading eyes to look far.

exEn: Wǒ chī fàn — I eat rice
strokeTip: Mouth radical (口) on left, then 乞 on right. Mouth + begging = eating.
Mouth (口) left, then 曷 right. A mouth open wide to drink.
Central vertical first, then left branch strokes, then right branch strokes — a river branching.
Food radical (饣) left, then 反 right. Food reversed — cooked and served.

exEn: Zhè běn shū hěn hǎo — This book is very good
strokeTip: Strokes form the shape of an open book with a brush stroke below.
Top part represents hands exchanging knowledge, child (子) at bottom — a child receiving learning.
Top strokes suggest grey hair, then the person below — an elder with grey hair.
Left radical, then the right side. The silk radical represents craft passed down.
Two moon (月) shapes side by side — two moons together, like two people side by side.
A hand reaching out (又) with an extra stroke — a hand extended in friendship.
Father (父) on top,巴 below. Father combined with 'ba' sound component.
Woman (女) left, 马 right. A woman + horse sound — mom.
Two 可 stacked — a mouth that can speak maturely, an elder brother.
Woman (女) left, 且 right — a woman who came before.
Grain (禾) simplified on top, then a person below — harvest cycle = one year.
A crescent moon shape — the original drawing of a moon with two inner strokes.
A box representing the sun with a line inside — the sun's core of light.
A person under a roof with a dot — someone present right now.

exEn: Jīntiān tiānqì hǎo — The weather is nice today
strokeTip: Big (大) with a stroke on top — above everything, that's the sky.
Movement radical on the outside, 文 inside — this thing in motion.
Left side is a cloth radical, right is 邑 (city) — that place over there.

exEn: Nǐ jiào shénme míngzi — What is your name?
strokeTip: Person (亻) left, 十 right — a person asking ten questions.
A small tail stroke — a soft ending sound, written lightly.
A table shape with two legs — like counting on a small table.
Two evening (夕) stacked — many evenings = a lot of time = many.
Small (小) with an extra stroke — even smaller, fewer.
Water radical (氵) left, 殳 right — water that has disappeared, nothing left.

exEn: Wǒ yǒu yīgè píngguǒ — I have an apple
strokeTip: A hand (又) reaching for meat (月/肉) — having something to hold.

exEn: Wǒ zài jiā — I am at home
strokeTip: Earth (土) with a left stroke — being planted somewhere on earth.

exEn: Wǒ hé nǐ — Me and you
strokeTip: Grain (禾) left, mouth (口) right — sharing food together = harmony.
White (白) left, spoon (勺) right. The most common character in Chinese — write it smoothly.
Child (子) simplified — two quick strokes, a small completion mark.

exEn: Nǐ hǎo ma — Are you well?
strokeTip: Mouth (口) left, 马 right — open your mouth to ask a question.

exEn: Wǒ xiǎng qù dànshì méi shíjiān — I want to go but have no time
strokeTip: Person (亻) left, 旦 right — a person at dawn, a new but different situation.
A box with 大 inside — a cause contained within borders.
A hand leading an elephant — doing something for a purpose.
Axe radical (斤) right — the place where things are cut and decided.
Woman (女) left, mouth (口) right — speaking as a woman would, softly comparing.
Field (田) on top of tree (木) — fruit growing on a tree in the field.
Sun (日) left, temple (寺) right — the sun marks time at the temple bell.
Person (亻) left, 侯 right — a person waiting by the side.
A mouth (口) open with a curved stroke above — permission given verbally.
A person standing with a tool — using something to get things done.

exEn: Wǒ yào qù — I want to go
strokeTip: West (西) on top, woman (女) below — a woman reaching for something in the west, wanting.

exEn: Wǒ xiǎng nǐ — I miss you
strokeTip: Mutual (相) on top, heart (心) below — the heart thinking of something mutual.
Arrow (矢) left, mouth (口) right — words that fly straight like an arrow, knowledge.
Movement (辶) radical wrapping, 首 (head) inside — the head leads the way.

exEn: Wǒ huì shuō zhōngwén — I can speak Chinese
strokeTip: Person under a roof with a cloud-like top — people gathering with skill.
Person (亻) left, then 故 right — a person doing something purposefully.
A bucket shape — a container that is put to use.
Silk radical (纟) left, 合 right — binding threads together, giving a gift.

exEn: Bǎ shū fàng xià — Put the book down
strokeTip: Hand (扌) left, 巴 right — a hand gripping a handle.
Two person (人) strokes — one following the other, from behind.
Inch (寸) left, doubled strokes right — measuring precisely = correct.
Run (走) left, 己 right — the self starting to run, rising up.
A person with moving legs — the strokes suggest a walking motion.
Two mountains stacked — something emerging and going up, exiting.
Movement radical (辶) wrapping, 井 (well) inside — entering toward the well.
A box within a box — turning and going back to the center.
Roof (宀) on top, pig (豕) below — a pig under the roof, the family livestock at home.
Field (田) on top, earth (土) below — inside the field, within.
A face shape — outer strokes frame the center, like a face.
Two hands (开) pulling a bolt open — gates opening wide.
Woman (女) left, 台 right — a woman at the platform, starting something.
Roof (宀) on top, 元 (origin) below — coming back to origin, complete.
A weapon stroke with a completed hook — a task struck through to completion.
A winding stroke representing something past — already done and gone.
Silk (纟) left, 巠 right — threads running through, passing continuously.

exEn: Hái yǒu — Also have / still have
strokeTip: Movement (辶) radical wrapping, 不 inside — still moving, not yet done.
Horizontal strokes over a cross — layering something again on top.
Eye (目) simplified with legs — an eye that can walk toward you.

exEn: Qǐngwèn — Excuse me / may I ask
strokeTip: Gate (门) with mouth (口) inside — a mouth at the door asking to come in.
Speech (讠) left, 青 (green/young) right — speaking kindly, a polite invitation.
Speech (讠) left, 射 right — words shot like arrows of gratitude.
學 simplified on top, see (见) below — seeing with learned awareness.
Mutual (咸) on top, heart (心) below — the heart sensing all around it.
A pregnant woman silhouette — the body carrying life within.
Person (亻) left, 本 (root) right — a person's fundamental form.
Curved base then three dots — the chambers of a beating heart.
A simplified head shape — strokes represent hair on top of a head.
Three horizontal strokes (fingers) crossed by a curved line (palm) — a hand.
Eye radical (目) left, 艮 right — an eye that holds its gaze firmly.
A simple box shape — the opening of a mouth drawn from above.
Insect (虫) on top — even though a small insect, still persistent.
Dog (犬) meat (月) on fire (灬) — something so natural it's like cooking.
A beard hanging down — suggesting connection and continuation.
A shelf shape — stacking things on top of each other, adding more.

exEn: Wǒ yě shì — I am too
strokeTip: A curving shape like a branch — reaching out in the same direction, also.
Movement (者) left, city (阝) right — all people in the city together.
Mouth (口) with a small stroke — a small mouth saying only one thing.
Capital (京) left, knife (刀) right — cutting straight to the point.
Clothing (衤) left, 皮 (skin) right — something worn on the outside, passive.

exEn: Ràng wǒ lái — Let me do it
strokeTip: Speech (讠) left, 上 right — speaking to let someone go ahead.
Two person (匕) shapes side by side — comparing two things next to each other.
Vehicle (车) left, 交 right — vehicles crossing paths, comparing routes.
A sun and a cross — the sun rising further, even more than before.
Sun (日) top, take (取) below — taking the highest point of the sun.
Two sets of strokes facing away from each other — opposing, not the same.
Cloth (巾) at bottom, 尚 above — a cloth skirt worn daily, ordinary.
Cow (牛) left, temple (寺) right — a special bull kept at the temple.
Mouth and sword (别) — a sharp word separating things apart.
A nose shape — pointing to yourself (Chinese point to nose to mean 'me').
A curled stroke representing wrapping around oneself — the self contained.
A mouth (口) under a cover sharing the same space — together, same.
Sound (音) on top, heart (心) below — the heart giving sound to meaning.
Field (田) on top, heart (心) below — the heart working through a field of thoughts.
Old (老) simplified, 丂 below — an elder being questioned, an exam.
Insect (虎) top, think (思) bottom — careful thought through all concerns.
Water (氵) left, 夬 right — water that has broken through a dam, decisive.
Roof (宀) on top, 正 (correct) below — a correct thing under a roof, settled.
Speech (讠) left, 十 (ten) right — speaking ten things, planning precisely.
A knife (刂) right, 戈 left — cutting a line through plans.
Water (氵) left, 去 right — water that flows in one direction, a rule.
A square boat shape — a flat vessel going in a clear direction.
Heart (忄) left, 青 (green/clear) right — a clear heart, pure feeling.
Water (氵) left, 兄 right — like water flowing around an elder, a condition.
Silk (纟) left, 吉 (lucky) right — tying lucky threads together, a knot.
Two hands operating a gate bar — closing something, a connection point.
Silk threads (纟) twisting together — threads that connect and bind.
Scenery (景) left, three strokes (彡) right representing light rays — a shadow cast.
Mouth (口) left, 乡 (countryside) right — sound echoing through the countryside.
Village (里) with an extra stroke — something weighty pressing down on the earth.
Is (是) left, page (页) right — the correct topic on the page.
Bamboo (⺮) top, 合 below — bamboo slips with answers written, a response.
Horn (角) left, knife (刀) and ox (牛) right — a knife cutting through an ox horn, untying.
Earth (王/土) left, village (里) right — managing the earth's principles.
Roof (宀) on top, 贯 below — something real and valuable under a roof.
Mound (阝) left, 祭 right — ceremonies at the border between states.
Eye (目) top, two hands (廾) below — hands holding something up to examine, a tool.
Cross (交) left, strike (攵) right — a strike that crosses, producing effect.
Center strokes suggest a standard measuring device — setting the rate.
Hand (扌) left, 是 right — a hand raising something up to present it.
Person (亻) left, 共 (together) right — a person supplying to everyone together.
Rain (雨) top, 而 below — rain that is needed, a necessity.
Water (水) with a hook — a hand scooping for water, seeking something.
Bamboo (⺮) top, official (官) below — bamboo pipes through which officials flow water.
Two strokes launching from a bow — an arrow being released, sending forth.
Corpse (尸) top, 共 below — something laid out and spread wide to see.
Pen (聿) on top, movement (辶) below — writing a plan then moving to build it.
A person standing on the ground — legs spread, firmly standing up.
Earth (土) left, 曾 right — earth piling up higher, increasing.
Power (力) right, mouth (口) left — strength added to words, adding more.
Water (氵) left, 咸 right — water decreasing as salt draws it out.
Two strokes at top, silk (糸) bottom — threads of change twisting into something new.
Person (亻) left, reversed person (匕) right — one person flipped, transformed.
Movement (辶) wrapping, 大 inside — a large stride reaching the destination.
Arrive (至) left, knife (刂) right — a blade arriving at a precise point.
A vertical eye shape — the original pictograph of a human eye.
Tree (木) left, 票 right — a mark put on a tree, a sign post.
Bamboo (⺮) top, 朿 below — bamboo strips used to write out strategy.
Field (田) left, each (各) right — skipping across each field, a shortcut plan.
Hand (扌) left, 丸 right — a hand gripping and holding firm.
A crossroads shape — two paths crossing, movement in multiple directions.
Speech (讠) left, 平 (flat) right — speaking a balanced, fair assessment.
Person (亻) left, 古 (ancient) right — an elder person making a wise estimate.
Stone (石) left, 隺 right — as solid and certain as a stone.
Speech (讠) left, 忍 right — words spoken with conviction, recognizing truth.
Person carrying a shell (贝) on their back — bearing a burden, taking responsibility.
Bundle (束) top, shell (贝) bottom — valuable things bundled, one's duty.
Ten (十) left, three forces (劦) right — ten strengths working in coordination.
Speech (讠) left, 义 (justice) right — speaking about what is just.
A lid (人) on top of a mouth (口) — fitting together perfectly, suitable.
Hand (扌) left, 比 right — a hand comparing and marking, approving in batches.
Ice (冫) left, 隹 right — a bird frozen in place, precise and accurate.
Speech (讠) left, 周 right — speaking around a topic to find the right tune.
Bundle (束) top, attack (攴) middle, correct (正) bottom — correcting through ordered effort.
Hand (扌) left, 艮 right — a hand delivering something important, reporting.
Ox (牛) top, mouth (口) below — an ox before a mouth, announcing solemnly.
Combine (囱) top, heart (心) below — the heart that holds everything together.
A branch shape with a hand below — a hand holding up a branch, supporting.
Hand (扌) left, temple (寺) right — a hand held at the temple, maintaining steadily.
Hand (扌) left, 隹 right — a hand pushing a bird forward.
